page: Microsoft Azure D16a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.35 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, the server includes 410 GB of local storage and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the D16a_v4 delivers strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of tested servers in stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core performance, however, aligns with the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also register average performance. Similarly, data decompression, software compilation, and database operations fall within the middle tier. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server exhibits strong text generation speeds with a small 135M parameter LLM, though prompt processing and larger model inference remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16a_v4 offers a balanced resource profile, combining dedicated AMD EPYC compute power with a standard memory-to-core ratio and bundled local storage. This configuration provides a stable, predictable performance baseline without the premium associated with specialized high-memory or GPU-accelerated instances. The server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate multi-threaded applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D16a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DAv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 410 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small language models, while multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at an average level. Lacking GPU acceleration, it offers a balanced resource profile with qualitative cost efficiency for standard workloads. It is suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16a_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage. It offers strong single-core performance for general workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16a_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and local storage for stable, everyday enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor at 3.35 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
410 GB of local storage bundled for temporary data needs
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation
Ideal for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and software compilation
